commit.message:
Update Dockerfile

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[doc/testplan] Add BMC high-level test plan (#22688)

* [doc/testplan] Add BMC high-level test plan

Add the high-level test plan for SONiC BMC testing, covering:
- Overview of SONiC BMC and its role
- Test scope (in scope and out of scope features)
- Two new testbed topologies: bmc-dual-mgmt and bmc-shared-mgmt
- Testbed modeling with devices, console links, and topology definitions
- Embedded ethernet switch VLAN configuration for bmc-shared-mgmt
- Sniffer device for VLAN tagging verification
- SonicHost extensions: is_bmc() and get_bmc_host()
- Reused test suites and bmc-shared-mgmt specific test cases

Signed-off-by: Jing Kan <jika@microsoft.com>

* Address review comments: sniffer clarification and any marker handling

- Expand sniffer-01 description: clarify pass-through behavior, no impact
  on mgmt traffic or CPU testing, shared hardware with fanout support
- Add sniffer port modeling in testbed.yaml with dut_facing_port and
  mgmt_facing_port fields
- Add subsection on handling the any topology marker using the existing
  conditional mark plugin for BMC-incompatible test exclusions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Jing Kan <jika@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
fix(net): harden outbound network stack (P0 + P1) (#98)

* fix(net): handle partial writes to guest FD

fd_write() can return fewer bytes than requested (short write). Both
callers — the writable drain loop and enqueue_or_write — ignored the
return value and discarded unwritten bytes, silently corrupting L2
frames delivered to the guest.

Now both paths check the written count: on partial write the unwritten
remainder is pushed to the front of the write queue for retry.

* fix(net): prevent DNS reply starvation under write backpressure

The reply_rx.recv() arm in the select! loop was gated by an
`if accept_replies` guard that disabled it entirely when the write
queue exceeded 512 frames. The fallback drain_reply_rx in the common
tail only drained 64 frames non-blocking — insufficient recovery.

Under sustained guest traffic with a full write queue, DNS reply tasks
blocked on the bounded reply channel (capacity 256), causing DNS
responses to be lost or indefinitely delayed.

Remove the write-queue gate: the bounded channel already provides
natural backpressure to spawned tasks. Remove the now-unused
WRITE_QUEUE_HIGH constant and its check in drain_reply_rx.

* fix(net): filter loopback and fake-IP from upstream DNS servers

On macOS, /etc/resolv.conf typically contains 127.0.0.1
(mDNSResponder). With Surge/Clash in fake-IP mode it may contain
198.18.0.x. Both break DNS forwarding: loopback may loop or fail,
fake-IP returns virtual addresses the guest cannot resolve.

Skip loopback (127.0.0.0/8, ::1) and the 198.18.0.0/15 fake-IP range
when parsing resolv.conf. When all servers are filtered, the existing
fallback to DEFAULT_UPSTREAM (8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1) activates.

* fix(net): add cancellation to spawned proxy tasks

All spawned network tasks (UDP relay, ICMP proxy, DNS forwarding) were
fire-and-forget with no cancellation mechanism. On datapath shutdown
they became orphans, holding sockets, buffers, and channel handles
until their individual timeouts (up to 60 s) expired.

Thread the existing CancellationToken through SocketProxy and into all
spawned tasks:
- UDP relay: cancel arm in the select loop
- ICMP proxy: early exit check before socket creation
- DNS forwarding: early exit check before upstream query

Move CancellationToken creation in the VMM before SocketProxy::new so
the same token is shared by the proxy and the datapath.

* fix(net): bounds-check DNS answer name-skip to prevent OOB read

The answer-section name-skipping loop in parse_dns_response_a_records
advanced offset by 1+len without verifying it stayed within the
buffer. A malformed DNS response with an invalid label length could
read out of bounds or silently corrupt the parse offset.

Return None (skip logging) when label length exceeds remaining data.

* fix(net): address PR #98 review comments

Partial write handling (comments 1-2):
- SOCK_DGRAM writes are atomic — a short write would corrupt frame
  boundaries. Change partial-write handling from re-queue-remainder to
  warn-and-drop, matching datagram semantics.

Cancellation promptness (comments 3-4):
- ICMP proxy: wrap the recv timeout in tokio::select! with
  cancel.cancelled() so shutdown doesn't wait the full 10 s.
- DNS forwarding: wrap forward_dns_async in tokio::select! with
  cancel.cancelled() so shutdown doesn't wait for upstream timeouts.

DNS upstream filtering (comment 6):
- Only filter loopback/fake-IP when better IPv4 alternatives exist.
  When resolv.conf has only 127.0.0.1 + IPv6 (common macOS default),
  keep loopback as fallback since forward_dns_async only binds IPv4.
- Skip IPv6-only servers entirely (unsupported by current forwarder).

Write queue backpressure (comment 7):
- Add WRITE_QUEUE_HARD_CAP (2048) to bound write_queue memory. Frames
  beyond the cap are dropped. reply_rx is still always polled so DNS
  tasks don't block, but memory growth is capped.

Misc:
- Fix dns_log test comment indentation (comment 5).

* fix(net): create per-SYN listen sockets for concurrent connections (#97)

* fix(net): create per-SYN listen sockets for concurrent connections

When multiple host connects for the same destination port complete in
the same poll_pending_syns batch, all injected SYN frames were sharing
a single smoltcp listen socket. smoltcp transitions a listen socket to
SYN-RECEIVED on the first accept, so subsequent SYNs to the same port
found no listener and received RST — surfacing as "connection refused"
in the guest.

This explains why `docker compose up -d` (concurrent pulls to :443)
failed while sequential `docker pull` worked fine.

Extract `create_listen_socket` from `ensure_listen_socket_for_port` and
call it unconditionally for each injected SYN, so every SYN gets its
own dedicated listener.

* fix(net): harden outbound network stack against resource leaks and edge cases

TcpBridge:
- Add `prune_excess_listen_sockets` to clean up surplus listen sockets
  created by `create_listen_socket`. Without this, each concurrent SYN
  batch leaves behind unconsumed LISTEN sockets (512 KiB each) that
  accumulate indefinitely in `port_handles`.
- Simplify `resolve_proxy_target`: remove dead-code fake-ip guard
  (unreachable after `has_usable_proxy()` early return) and use HTTPS
  proxy for all ports, not just 443 — HTTP CONNECT works on any port.

DNS forwarding:
- Validate that the DNS response transaction ID matches the query before
  accepting it, preventing stale/mismatched UDP responses.

Cleanup timers:
- Replace `Instant::checked_sub().unwrap()` with
  `duration_since().as_secs()` in UDP flow cleanup (socket_proxy,
  inbound_relay) to avoid a panic if the daemon starts within 60 s of
  system boot.

* test(net): add test for listen socket pruning

* docs: add network stack audit issue tracker

* fix(net): address PR review comments on tcp_bridge

- Remove dead `ensure_listen_socket_for_port` (no remaining call sites
  after the create_listen_socket refactor).
- Restore proxy support for non-fake-IP destinations when an explicit
  system proxy (HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS) is configured. The previous change
  dropped this, breaking corporate proxy environments.
- Add regression test: two SYNs to the same dst port completing in one
  poll_pending_syns batch must produce zero RSTs.

* fea

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style(net): cargo fmt dns_log test

* chore: remove issues.net.md

* fix(net): address latest PR review comments

- Short datagram write: escalate from warn to error — this is an
  invariant violation on SOCK_DGRAM, not a recoverable condition.
- DNS upstream fallback: unconditionally exclude 198.18.0.0/15 fake-IP
  even in the loopback-fallback path. When only fake-IP entries remain,
  return empty so DnsConfig::new keeps DEFAULT_UPSTREAM (8.8.8.8,
  1.1.1.1).

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(net): create per-SYN listen sockets for concurrent connections (#97)

* fix(net): create per-SYN listen sockets for concurrent connections

When multiple host connects for the same destination port complete in
the same poll_pending_syns batch, all injected SYN frames were sharing
a single smoltcp listen socket. smoltcp transitions a listen socket to
SYN-RECEIVED on the first accept, so subsequent SYNs to the same port
found no listener and received RST — surfacing as "connection refused"
in the guest.

This explains why `docker compose up -d` (concurrent pulls to :443)
failed while sequential `docker pull` worked fine.

Extract `create_listen_socket` from `ensure_listen_socket_for_port` and
call it unconditionally for each injected SYN, so every SYN gets its
own dedicated listener.

* fix(net): harden outbound network stack against resource leaks and edge cases

TcpBridge:
- Add `prune_excess_listen_sockets` to clean up surplus listen sockets
  created by `create_listen_socket`. Without this, each concurrent SYN
  batch leaves behind unconsumed LISTEN sockets (512 KiB each) that
  accumulate indefinitely in `port_handles`.
- Simplify `resolve_proxy_target`: remove dead-code fake-ip guard
  (unreachable after `has_usable_proxy()` early return) and use HTTPS
  proxy for all ports, not just 443 — HTTP CONNECT works on any port.

DNS forwarding:
- Validate that the DNS response transaction ID matches the query before
  accepting it, preventing stale/mismatched UDP responses.

Cleanup timers:
- Replace `Instant::checked_sub().unwrap()` with
  `duration_since().as_secs()` in UDP flow cleanup (socket_proxy,
  inbound_relay) to avoid a panic if the daemon starts within 60 s of
  system boot.

* test(net): add test for listen socket pruning

* docs: add network stack audit issue tracker

* fix(net): address PR review comments on tcp_bridge

- Remove dead `ensure_listen_socket_for_port` (no remaining call sites
  after the create_listen_socket refactor).
- Restore proxy support for non-fake-IP destinations when an explicit
  system proxy (HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS) is configured. The previous change
  dropped this, breaking corporate proxy environments.
- Add regression test: two SYNs to the same dst port completing in one
  poll_pending_syns batch must produce zero RSTs.

* fea

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: address code review findings

- Remove unused $current_url from bottom-nav.php (was using unsanitized $_SERVER)
- Replace date() with gmdate() in customizer.php and footer-content.php
- Add appstorepro_allowed_svg_kses() helper and use wp_kses() for SVG output
  in categories-section.php to prevent any potential XSS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add AppStore Pro WordPress theme

Complete WordPress theme for an Android APK store with:
- App CPT with full meta box (version, size, developer, download URL, etc.)
- app-category taxonomy
- Dark/light mode toggle with localStorage persistence
- 6-color theme picker (Green, Blue, Orange, Purple, Pink, Yellow)
- Particle canvas background with toggle
- Scroll progress bar
- Fixed header with search dropdown
- Mobile bottom navigation bar
- Slide-up menu panel with appearance controls
- Single app template: hero, screenshots carousel, Telegram card,
  download button, details table, YouTube tutorial accordion,
  related apps, sticky bottom bar
- Homepage: hero section, categories grid, horizontal scroll new apps,
  games section, all apps grid
- Reveal animations with IntersectionObserver
- WordPress Customizer integration
- Full CSS (40KB) with CSS variables, dark mode, responsive breakpoints
- Full JS (17KB) with all interactive features
- Android SVG icon asset
- All PHP properly escaped (esc_html, esc_url, esc_attr)
- Nonce verification on meta box saves

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(site): Just the Docs theme, domain guides, workflow recipes

- Switch _config.yml to just-the-docs/just-the-docs@v0.10.0 remote theme
- Add index.md as Jekyll homepage (README.md stays for GitHub.com viewers)
- Add experiments/index.md: AX/RX/DX summary with reproduce instructions
- Add domains/index.md + 5 domain guides: FINTECH, ML, GAME, CREATIVE, CLI
  - Each covers: active gates, domain-specific gate targets, verification strategy, spec patterns
  - AI-rubric evaluation type introduced in CREATIVE guide (RM-054 design)
- Add docs/recipes/index.md + 8 workflow recipes:
  - new-project-with-spec, new-project-no-spec, post-setup-first-cycle
  - brownfield-new-feature, bug-fix, migration, project-takeover, post-hardening
  - All reference forgecraft_actions tool with exact action names
  - Full TDD cycle, cascade, close_cycle, start_hardening steps included

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(white-paper): add Jacquard/foundry analogy to plain language summary

The craft relocation argument: Jacquard loom (unique output from spec),
foundry (quality upstream), Babbage's insight (punch card -> program).
Places GS in the historical arc of specification-as-primary-artifact.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: generative-specification

commit.message:
fix(white-paper): close two remaining linguistic attack surfaces

1. Abstract/body: 'what Chomsky classifies as context-sensitive reading'
   -> 'what Chomsky's hierarchy classifies as context-sensitive'
   Chomsky classifies grammars and languages, not reading acts.

2. §4.2: Add usage note that syntactic/semantic are used in the
   programming-language sense throughout, not the formal linguistics
   sense. Closes the gap for any linguist who would object to the
   borrowed terminology being used with different referents.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: clean up resource navigation and strip bulk data from JSON views (#19)

* fix: clean up resource navigation and strip bulk data from JSON views

1. PipelineInspector: strip moduleId from stage JSON (already shown
   as clickable ResourceLink in section title — was duplicated)

2. ResourceDetail: filterBulkFields() strips dataBase64, code,
   previewDataUrl, facePreviewUrls before passing to JsonTree.
   These are already shown by dedicated viewers (hex dump, shader
   editor, image preview) — no need to render as raw JSON walls.

3. ResourceInspector: delegate to ResourceDetail component instead
   of duplicating the category-branching logic inline. Ensures
   bulk field filtering applies everywhere.

All resource IDs (moduleId, textureId, layoutId, pipelineId, etc.)
remain clickable via ResourceLink auto-detection in JsonTree.

* fix: remove false [Circular] markers, all resource IDs are links

Root cause: WeakSet for circular detection was shared across React
re-renders. Sibling objects got marked as 'visited' from previous
render passes, showing [Circular] on perfectly valid data.

Fix: removed WeakSet entirely. Capture data is serialized JSON with
no actual circular references. MAX_DEPTH=10 provides sufficient
protection against deep nesting.

Tests updated:
- Verify same-shaped sibling objects do NOT show [Circular]
- Verify shared object references do NOT show [Circular]
- Verify actual circular refs don't crash (MAX_DEPTH protection)
- Verify resource ID values in object keys are present for linking

* fix: add ResourceLink to all ID/label displays

Every place showing a resource ID now has a clickable link:
- BufferDetail: buffer ID is a ResourceLink
- PipelineInspector: pipeline ID is a ResourceLink + label
- ShaderModuleDetail: shader ID is a ResourceLink + label
- ShaderViewer: shader moduleId linked in section title
- JsonTree: auto-links all string values matching resource ID pattern

Navigate from any view to any resource with a single click.

* feat: serialize GPU object IDs in command args for clickable links

* fix: remove unused useMemo import from JsonTree

* docs: update specs with GPU object serialization and JsonTree details

capture-engine.md:
- New 'Argument Serialization' section: IdResolver, __id field,
  GPU object format, where resolver is passed, bulk field filtering,
  no-circular-ref rationale

ui-components.md:
- JsonTree: auto-link pattern, GPU object compact rendering,
  no WeakSet, bulk filtering applied by ResourceDetail

* feat: 'Used By' cross-reference section on every resource

Shows all commands and resources that reference the selected resource,
with clickable links for instant navigation.

New usageIndex.ts builds a Map<resourceId, UsageEntry[]> from:
- Command tree: pipelineId, vertexBuffers, indexBufferId, bindGroups,
  and __id fields in serialized args
- Resource graph: pipelines->shaders, bind groups->buffers/views,
  texture views->textures

Index computed once per capture (useMemo). Lookup is O(1).
UsedBySection component shows resource links (clickable) and
command labels, appended at the bottom of every resource detail.

* fix: deep-scan command args for resource __id references in usage index

The usageIndex scanCommands() function only checked top-level keys of
node.args for __id fields, but serialized GPU object references are
nested at arbitrary depth inside arrays and objects (e.g. beginRenderPass
descriptor → colorAttachments → view → __id).

Replace the shallow key scan with a recursive collectIds() walker that
finds all __id strings at any nesting depth. This fixes missing 'used in'
navigation links for resources referenced by beginRenderPass,
copyBufferToBuffer, copyTextureToTexture, writeBuffer, setPipeline,
setBindGroup, setVertexBuffer, and other commands.

Adds comprehensive unit tests for buildUsageIndex.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: deep-scan buffer Used By with collectIds, command links, and component tests

- Add collectIds() to usageIndex.ts: recursively walks serialized args
  trees to find all __id references (writeBuffer, copyBufferToBuffer,
  beginRenderPass descriptors, etc.)
- Replace shallow top-level key scan with deep collectIds traversal
- Export UsedBySection for testability
- Add CommandLink component and CommandNavigationContext for clickable
  command entries in Used By sections
- Add navigateToCommand callback in ResultApp (DFS find, mode switch,
  history push)
- Update specs: architecture.md component tree, ui-components.md with
  UsedBySection, CommandLink, CommandNavigationContext, and usageIndex docs
- Add 6 new tests: writeBuffer args, aggregate buffer refs, and 4
  UsedBySection component rendering tests (empty, commands, resources, mixed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(white-paper): linguistic precision fixes from philologist review

Six targeted corrections based on expert critique:

1. grammatical vs valid: Chomsky uses 'grammatical' not 'valid'. The
   paper now distinguishes Chomsky's structural notion (grammaticality)
   from GS's broader obligation (validity = grammatical + behaviorally
   conformant). The extension is explicit, not elided.

2. Load-bearing analogy: Added explicit mapping table — specification
   is the grammar, codebase is the language, implementation artifacts
   are sentences, architectural violations are ungrammatical strings,
   constraints are production rules, AI derivation is the generation
   process. States clearly what the analogy does NOT import (formal
   apparatus, derivation trees, pumping lemma).

3. generative capacity -> derivation precision: 'Generative capacity'
   in formal language theory is a property of the formalism type
   (Type 3 < Type 2 < Type 1), not of specific grammars. Replaced
   throughout with 'derivation precision', which names what the paper
   actually claims. Restriction glossary entry updated with explicit
   parenthetical clarifying the distinction.

4. Generative grammar definition: Previous definition described any
   formal grammar. Revised to capture what is specific to Chomsky's
   notion — recursive generativity, competence modeling, structural
   account of grammaticality. Explicitly states what formal apparatus
   is NOT being imported.

5. Context-free/context-sensitive glossary entries: Added explicit
   'used here as structural analogy' disclaimer so the analogical
   register is clear.

6. Footnote 2: Updated vocabulary list from 'valid' to 'grammatical'
   with a cross-reference to §4.1.a's explicit extension note.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Raise on failed individual asset generation to prevent incorrect cache updates (#1130)

* Initial plan

* Add error handling for failed image generation and tests to verify behavior

- In generate.py, each individual_* function now logs an error and raises
  an Exception when core does not produce the expected output file. This
  ensures the failure propagates up through generate_assets() so the asset
  type is not added to the successful_assets list and the cache is not
  incorrectly updated.
- Add tests/test_generate.py with 8 tests covering all four individual_*
  functions, verifying both the failure case (raises) and success case
  (no raise).

Co-authored-by: oscarlevin <6504596+oscarlevin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rebase on main, run black formatting, add CHANGELOG entry

- Rebased on origin/main (includes new tests and changes from #1124-#1128)
- Ran `poetry run black .` for formatting (reformatted test_generate.py)
- Added Fixed entry in CHANGELOG.md under [Unreleased]

commit.message:
fix: preserve session cwd across /new and session_expired

resetSessionUI() clears state.sessionCwd to null before
requestNewSession() reads it, so new sessions always fell back
to defaultCwd from config instead of inheriting the previous
session's working directory.

Capture cwd before calling resetSessionUI() in all affected
paths: /new command, session_expired event handler, and
connection resume fallbacks.
